title: Snowball cookies
subtitle: Mexican wedding cookies rolled in icing sugar
excerpt: Small, round, pecan-and-butter cookies rolled in icing sugar while warm, then rolled again when cool. They crumble at the bite and taste primarily of browned butter and toasted nut.
sourceNotes: Mexican wedding cookies and Russian tea cakes are the same biscuit. The recipe appears under both names in mid-twentieth-century American domestic cookery. The technique (a short, nut-studded butter dough rolled in icing sugar) has cognates in many European traditions (Greek kourabiedes, Italian polverone). The double-rolling (warm then cool) is documented from the 1950s onwards.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze without the icing-sugar coating. Roll in icing sugar after defrosting.

glossaryTerms.definition: Heating raw nuts briefly in a dry pan or oven until fragrant and lightly coloured. The heat drives off surface moisture and triggers the Maillard reaction, developing flavour compounds that raw nuts lack.

content.text: These are one of the most forgiving biscuits to make: no rolling, no cutters, no icing. Roll the dough into balls, bake, roll in icing sugar. The double-rolling matters: the first coat while the cookies are still warm sticks to the surface and forms a base layer; the second coat when they are cold is what gives them the thick, snowy finish.
Toast the pecans before chopping them.
takes 8 minutes in the oven and makes a noticeable difference to the final flavour.
Heat the oven to 180°C fan. Spread the {{pecans}} on a baking tray and toast for 7–8 minutes until they smell nutty and are lightly browned. Cool completely, then chop roughly.

Beat the {{unsalted-butter}} and 50 g of {{icing-sugar}} (for the dough) together until pale and fluffy. Beat in the {{vanilla-extract}}. Add the {{plain-flour}} and {{fine-sea-salt}} and mix until just combined. Fold in the chopped pecans. Refrigerate for 30 minutes.

Roll the dough into balls about 3 cm in diameter — roughly a heaped teaspoon each. Arrange on lined baking trays with 3 cm between them. Bake for 12–14 minutes until the cookies are set and very pale gold on the base. They should not brown on top.

Put the 150 g of {{icing-sugar}} for rolling in a shallow bowl. While the cookies are still warm, roll them gently in the icing sugar to coat. They will be fragile — handle carefully. Leave to cool completely on a rack, then roll again in the icing sugar for a thick, white finish.
